assertions.title: Audio Transcription Dispatches Output Types from Multipart Uploads

steps.action: Review the prior mocked flow report for VAL-MEDIA-004
steps.expected: Only the transcription `text` and `srt` variants remain unproven
steps.observed: `mock_audio.json` already proved multipart file fidelity, model and optional-field forwarding, and typed dispatch for `json`, `verbose_json`, `vtt`, and `diarized_json`; it blocked only the missing `text` and `srt` clauses.

steps.action: Run the external probe test `transcription_text_and_srt_variants_dispatch_typed_bodies` against the path dependency
steps.expected: `response_format=text` returns `TranscriptionResponse::Text`, `response_format=srt` returns `TranscriptionResponse::Srt`, and both requests stay multipart uploads to `/v1/audio/transcriptions` with preserved file bytes
steps.observed: The probe passed. `text` returned the exact plain-text body, `srt` returned the exact subtitle body, both responses exposed the scripted request IDs, and the loopback server captured multipart `POST /v1/audio/transcriptions` requests with the expected filename, content type, raw bytes, and `response_format` fields (`text` and `srt`).

evidence.network: Loopback mock server captured 2 successful `POST /v1/audio/transcriptions` multipart requests.

assertions.reason: Combining the prior `mock_audio.json` evidence with this probe closes the remaining VAL-MEDIA-004 gap: multipart upload semantics and the other typed formats were already proven, and this probe proves the missing `text` and `srt` transcription variants through the SDK user surface.

assertions.title: Audio Translation Returns English Output Across Structured and Text Formats

steps.action: Review the prior mocked flow report for VAL-MEDIA-006
steps.expected: Only the translation `text` and `vtt` variants remain unproven
steps.observed: `mock_audio.json` already proved multipart file fidelity, `prompt`/`temperature` forwarding, and typed dispatch for structured `{ text }`, `verbose_json`, and `srt`; it blocked only the missing `text` and `vtt` clauses.

steps.action: Run the external probe test `translation_text_and_vtt_variants_dispatch_typed_bodies` against the path dependency
steps.expected: `response_format=text` returns `TranslationResponse::Text`, `response_format=vtt` returns `TranslationResponse::Vtt`, and both requests stay multipart uploads to `/v1/audio/translations` with preserved file bytes
steps.observed: The probe passed. `text` returned the exact plain-text body, `vtt` returned the exact subtitle body, both responses exposed the scripted request IDs, and the loopback server captured multipart `POST /v1/audio/translations` requests with the expected filename, content type, raw bytes, `prompt`/`temperature`, and `response_format` fields (`text` and `vtt`).

evidence.network: Loopback mock server captured 2 successful `POST /v1/audio/translations` multipart requests.

assertions.reason: Combining the prior `mock_audio.json` evidence with this probe closes the remaining VAL-MEDIA-006 gap: multipart upload semantics plus structured and `srt` formats were already proven, and this probe proves the missing `text` and `vtt` translation variants through the SDK user surface.

commands.observation: The temporary external probe compiled `openai-rust` by path and passed both assigned tests: `transcription_text_and_srt_variants_dispatch_typed_bodies` and `translation_text_and_vtt_variants_dispatch_typed_bodies`.

summary: Ran a validation-only external Cargo probe and resolved the remaining format gaps for both assigned assertions. VAL-MEDIA-004 now has explicit `text` and `srt` transcription proof, and VAL-MEDIA-006 now has explicit `text` and `vtt` translation proof, so both assertions are marked pass when combined with the prior `mock_audio.json` coverage.
